title: [Task Sprint] QA Testing Report — GoThru Photo Studio Tauri

issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-19.17-29-47.mp4

Overview

Migrate GoThru Photo Studio from Electron to Tauri v2 + Rust. Epic tracking the
phased migration. Fresh install — no migration of old user data (DB/settings start empty;
no NeDB importer).

Goal: smaller bundle, lower idle memory, tighter security (no full Node runtime in the renderer).

Strategy (current — supersedes the original "sidecars + Node bridge" plan)

Direct port to native Rust, feature-by-feature. The renderer is carried over as-is
(React 18 + TS strict, Tailwind v4 + HeroUI, Redux Toolkit, i18next, react-toastify);
the only renderer change is the bridge: window.electron.* → Tauri invoke/listen
(centralized in src/lib/electron-bridge.ts).

The Node sidecar is skipped by default — light channels are ported straight to Rust.
A sidecar / bundled external binary is used only for genuinely heavy channels
(360 binaries, exiftool write, AI image generation). This is a planned deviation from the
original Strategy A, kept consistent with the bundle/memory/security goal.

  • UX parity is mandatory (flow + output identical to Electron).
  • Code flow is free to improve — the Electron code is reference for what, not how
    (it has real bugs/anti-patterns; do not port those).

Migration surface (verified)

~168 IPC channels (142 registrations; the message dispatcher expands to 24 sub-types).
Backend split by phase target: core (Tauri plugins) ~15 · rust (port required) ~85 ·
sidecar (external binary / AI) ~40.

Phases

  • P0 — Inventory & docs — IPC inventory, per-feature docs, architecture map, stack/skills.
  • P1 — Scaffold — Tauri v2 shell; renderer moved to src/renderer; bridge window.electron.*
    → invoke/listen; Rust backend + plugins registered; store → RTK; app boots & UI renders.
  • P2 — Node sidecar — intentionally skipped (replaced by direct-to-Rust). Sidecar/bundled
    binary added only per heavy channel as needed.
  • P3 — Port to Rust, per feature — all features ported to native Rust (see checklist).
  • P4 — Cutover & cleanup — retire Electron, finalize updater/signing/packaging (Win + mac).
  • Final pass — UI polish on HeroUI (visual/a11y) once function + UX are solid.

✅ Features completed via AI

"AI" = ported/implemented by the assistant. Manual test boxes are left unchecked —
@esa08 will verify and tick these.

Feature AI ported Manual test
App shell (updater, first-run, deep-link, native menu, theme, lifecycle) ✅ [ ]
Auth — Google OAuth (login, account/log manager, API-key checks) ✅ ✅
Preference / Settings (all setters, store + SQLite) ✅ ✅
File Manager (list/watch, create/rename/move/copy/delete, dialogs, drag) ✅ ✅
Path / OS / System (home dir, special paths, clipboard, online, devtools) ✅ ✅
Preview / 360 Viewer (thumbnails, image view, RAW preview, 360 video detect) ✅ ✅
Metadata / EXIF / GPS (read EXIF, GPS read/write, dimensions, reverse-geocode) ✅ [ ]
Map (GPS markers, satellite tile source, custom URI scheme) ✅ [ ]
Cache (clear/clear-all, info, total size, prune unused thumbs) ✅ ✅
Favorites & Bookmarks (SQLite) ✅ [ ]
Upload / Publish (GoThru, Panorra, Google Street View) ✅ ✅
Edit › Rotate image (single + batch) ✅ ✅
Edit › Image straightener / equiRotate ✅ ✅
Edit › Image Resize (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Minify JPG (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Convert 360 (equirect ↔ cube faces, incl. face preview) ✅ ✅
Edit › Enfuse (HDR merge) ✅ ✅
Edit › Nadir Logo Patching & Builder ✅ ✅
Edit › Auto Level (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › AI Nadir Patching (queue) ✅ ✅
Edit › Room Staging (AI) ✅ ✅
Edit › Virtual Staging 2D (AI) ✅ ✅
Edit › Eqroll (eqroll / eqroll-multiple) ✅ ✅
Edit › Eq2photo capture ✅ ✅
Edit › Scale Up Image ✅ ✅

Acceptance / cutover criteria

  1. All IPC channels have a verified Tauri/Rust (or justified sidecar) equivalent.
  2. UX + output parity confirmed vs Electron (viewer, map, every Edit tool).
  3. Updater + signing + notarization working on Win + mac.
  4. Installer size + idle memory measurably better than Electron.

Results — why the Tauri build is better

Installer / build size

  • Electron: ~300 MB — every Electron app bundles a full Chromium + Node.js runtime.
  • Tauri: ~80 MB — uses the OS-native webview (WebView2 on Windows, WKWebView on macOS) instead of shipping its own browser.
  • Still shrinkable in upcoming builds:
    • Rust release-profile optimization (strip, opt-level = "z", lto, panic = "abort"),
    • trim sidecar binaries to per-platform only (e.g. exiftool),
    • drop unused dependencies and optimize large assets (images/SVG),
    • code-split the renderer bundle.

Other gains

  • Lower idle memory — no bundled Chromium + Node processes running behind the UI.
  • Rust backend — heavy ops (directory listing, thumbnail decode/cache, rotate, 360 convert) are native: faster, memory-safe, with controlled parallelism (prewarm + semaphore) instead of a single Node event loop.
  • Tighter security — the renderer has no direct Node/fs access; everything goes through invoke/events gated by explicit Tauri capabilities. Much smaller attack surface than Electron's Node-in-renderer model.
  • Correct native integration per OS — native menu on macOS (global menu bar); HTML menu on Windows to avoid the WebView2 viewport-reflow flicker the native menu caused; native dialogs/clipboard/deep-link/notifications via Tauri plugins.
  • Lighter distribution & updates — a much smaller download for first install and auto-update.

Directly advances the issue Goal (smaller bundle, lower idle memory, tighter security) and acceptance criterion #4 (installer size + idle memory measurably better than Electron).

QA Tester Summary

Suite / Environment / Overall Result

Field Value
Suite Electron to Tauri migration epic, Issue #6
Environment Windows installed GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2; source contract review; Playwright scoped Windows contract
Test Date 2026-07-02
Overall Result Scoped Windows validation passed; full cutover acceptance remains open

Test Scenario & Result

TC Scenario Result Evidence / Notes
TC1 Verify Tauri app shell, plugin setup, SQLite initialization, and broad command registry are present Passed Source contract confirms Tauri builder/plugins are registered and the command registry contains at least 120 command entries, including file manager, settings, metadata, edit queues, Enfuse, upload, and AI staging groups. Installed app launches cleanly on Windows.
TC2 Verify renderer bridge maps legacy window.electron calls to Tauri invoke/listen Passed src/lib/electron-bridge.ts preserves the legacy renderer API while routing request/response and event flows through Tauri APIs.
TC3 Verify Windows installed-app cutover scope and package config Passed No Electron runtime dependency is present in the active package dependencies. Tauri bundle config is active, and the installed Windows executable path is configured as gothru-photo-studio-tauri.exe.
TC4 Review security, parity, updater/signing/notarization scope Passed for scoped documentation; not a full cutover pass Tauri capability and migration limitation checks are documented. macOS runtime, signing/notarization, full updater cutover, and full golden Electron UX/output parity were not executed in this Windows-only pass.
TC5 Installed Windows app smoke recording and responsiveness Passed The installed app launched, switched to Edit tools, returned to File Manager/source area, remained responsive, and was closed after recording. Memory snapshot during recording: Working Set 51,372,032 bytes; Private Memory 10,379,264 bytes.

Bug Notes

  • No blocking runtime bug was found in the scoped Windows app shell, bridge/package contract, or installed-app smoke flow.
  • This QA pass does not claim final epic cutover completion because macOS runtime, Win/mac signing and notarization, full updater cutover, and golden Electron parity were outside this scoped run.
  • Broad file-system capability scope is visible in the Tauri capability config and should remain part of security review, but it is not filed as a bug in this QA pass because photo-folder access may require broad user-directory coverage.

Code Review

  • src-tauri/src/lib.rs uses the Tauri v2 builder, plugin registration, SQLite init, and a large command registry covering migrated feature groups.
  • src/lib/electron-bridge.ts keeps the carried-over renderer contract by translating legacy window.electron calls to Tauri invoke, listen, and asset conversion APIs.
  • src-tauri/capabilities/default.json explicitly scopes Tauri permissions instead of exposing a full Node runtime to the renderer.
  • Updater plugin activation/final signing/notarization are not proven by this scoped Windows contract and should be validated in the P4 cutover release workflow.

Response Code Review

  • Added Issue #6 scoped Windows migration contract coverage in qa-testing/automation/e2e/module/migration/issue-6-tauri-migration-scoped-windows.spec.ts.
  • Test execution result: 4 passed.
  • Installed-app recording script closes GoThru Photo Studio after capture and keeps video below the 10 MB upload/request limit.

White / Gray / Black Box Coverage

Method Coverage
White Box Verified Tauri builder, plugins, command registry, package dependencies, bundle config, and capability declarations.
Gray Box Verified the legacy renderer bridge contract and documented scoped limitations against the migration acceptance criteria.
Black Box Launched the installed Windows app, captured app-only screenshots/video, navigated to Edit tools and File Manager/source area, and checked responsiveness.

Confusion Matrix

Classification Result
True Positive Contract tests would catch missing core Tauri setup, missing bridge mappings, reintroduced Electron runtime dependency, or undocumented full-cutover limitations.
True Negative The scoped Windows shell/bridge/package/app-smoke checks found no blocking defect in the executed scope.
False Positive Risk Medium: full cutover gaps are scope limitations for this pass, not automatic bugs, so they should not be treated as failed behavior without dev/tester confirmation.
False Negative Risk Medium-high: macOS, signing/notarization, updater release behavior, and golden output parity across every migrated Edit tool still require separate evidence before closing the epic as fully accepted.

Evidence

Installed App Screenshots

TC1 - Installed Tauri app shell launches cleanly on Windows and shows GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2 with the migrated main UI.

TC2 - Edit tools are available in the installed Windows app, including migrated edit modules such as Enfuse, resize, minifier, leveling, staging, and AI tools.

TC3 - File Manager/source area remains accessible after the Edit tool view, showing the carried-over app navigation flow.

Installed App Video

Clean scoped Windows recording: installed app launches, navigates through migrated app shell areas, remains responsive, and is closed after capture.

issue-6-tauri-migration-scoped-windows-20260702-104737.mp4

Contract / Measurement Artifacts

TC1-TC4 - Playwright scoped Windows migration contract run: 4 passed.

Command: qa-testing\scripts\playwright-local.cmd test --config qa-testing\automation\e2e\playwright.config.ts --grep @issue-6

Installed app size measurement: 116,437,156 bytes across 529 files in C:\Users\Work\AppData\Local\GoThru Photo Studio.

Installed app responsiveness/memory snapshot: app was responding during capture; Working Set 51,372,032 bytes and Private Memory 10,379,264 bytes at snapshot time.

Final QA Decision

Scoped Windows validation passed. Keep Issue #6 open as a migration epic until full cutover acceptance is validated with macOS runtime, Windows/macOS signing and notarization, updater release workflow, and golden Electron UX/output parity evidence.

issues.display_title: Issue #8: Handle Issue File Sorting by "Name" is Not Respected for .pts Files
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.17-47-16.mp4

A bug has been identified in the file manager UI where sorting by "Name" does not correctly apply to all file types.

When a user clicks the "Name" column header to sort files alphabetically (ascending), the .jpg files sort correctly at the top. However, the .pts files are entirely segregated and pushed to the bottom of the grid, failing to interleave alphabetically with the rest of the files. It appears the system is either applying an unintended primary sort by file extension, or the .pts files are completely bypassing the alphabetical sorting logic.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When the "Name" column is set to ascending/descending, all files in the directory sort strictly alphabetically by their filenames.
  2. .pts files are not artificially grouped at the bottom; they must appear in their correct alphabetical position alongside .jpg files.
  3. Toggling the sort direction updates the order correctly for all file extensions.

📊 Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: 🟡 Medium — Quick win.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
Med Med (1×) High ~0.5 pm Quick win

Engineering hypothesis: Comparator bug — the sort is likely applying an unintended primary sort by file extension (or .pts bypasses the alphabetical comparator entirely). Logic is isolated, so the fix is contained. Ensure the comparator sorts strictly by filename across all extensions and that toggling direction reverses uniformly (AC #1–#3).

QA Tester Summary - Issue #8: Handle Issue File Sorting by "Name" is Not Respected for .pts Files

Suite: qa-testing/scripts/record-issue-8-real-app-cdp-video.cjs (Real installed Tauri app via WebView2 CDP + clean app-surface MP4)
Env: GoThru Photo Studio Tauri 1.1.2 installed app (C:\Users\Work\AppData\Local\GoThru Photo Studio\gothru-photo-studio-tauri.exe)
Test date: 2026-07-02 16:35 WIB
Fixture: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-8/fixtures/mixed-name-sort-video-20260702-093517
Evidence manifest: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-8/artifacts/issue-8-real-app-video-manifest-20260702-093517.json
Video: Clean app-surface recording, no browser/navbar, duration 00:00:07.33.

Test Scenario & Result

# Method Scenario Given -> Expected Actual Matrix Result
1 White Box Sort contract and renderer sequence check Backend load-files with sortObject: { keyname: 1 } should return one shared filename order via sequence; renderer should merge .jpg and .pts by sequence Returned/merged order: alpha.jpg, bravo.pts, charlie.jpg, delta.pts, echo.jpg, foxtrot.pts, golf.jpg TP Pass
2 Gray Box Real app File Manager ascending Name sort Mixed .jpg + .pts folder -> strict alphabetical ascending by filename File Manager displayed .pts interleaved between .jpg: alpha.jpg, bravo.pts, charlie.jpg, delta.pts, echo.jpg, foxtrot.pts, golf.jpg TP Pass
3 Gray Box Real app File Manager descending Name sort Same folder with sortObject: { keyname: -1 } -> full reverse order across all extensions File Manager displayed: golf.jpg, foxtrot.pts, echo.jpg, delta.pts, charlie.jpg, bravo.pts, alpha.jpg TP Pass
4 Black Box End-user visual evidence context Screenshot and video must show real app File Manager, active fixture path, and Issue #8 mixed extension filenames Screenshots and MP4 show File Manager active and fixture path mixed-name-sort-video-20260702-093517; no Preview/Issue #7 stale context TP Pass

Acceptance Criteria Review

Acceptance Criteria Evidence Result
Name ascending/descending sorts all files strictly alphabetically by filename Manifest ac1StrictAlphabeticalAsc and ac3ToggleDescending are both true Pass
.pts files are not grouped at the bottom and interleave with .jpg Ascending order places bravo.pts, delta.pts, and foxtrot.pts at indices 1, 3, and 5 between .jpg files Pass
Toggling sort direction reverses order correctly for all extensions Descending actual array equals exact reverse of ascending expected array Pass

Backend / IPC Status Review

Channel / Layer Input Observed Output Review
Tauri command via renderer IPC load-files viewer: file-manager, panelSide: left, sortObject: { keyname: 1 } Payload contained 4 images and 3 regular files with shared sequence order 0..6 Verified
Tauri command via renderer IPC load-files viewer: file-manager, panelSide: left, sortObject: { keyname: -1 } Payload contained shared reverse sequence order 0..6 Verified
Renderer File Manager reducer regularFiles + images sorted by sequence UI displayed mixed extension order matching backend sequence Verified
Evidence recorder App-surface frames encoded to MP4 MP4 created successfully, 00:00:07.33, 22 frames captured before encoding Verified

Evidence Files

Type File
Screenshot qa-testing/reports/screenshots/issue-8-real-app-sort-video-20260702-093517-tc1-name-ascending.png
Screenshot qa-testing/reports/screenshots/issue-8-real-app-sort-video-20260702-093517-tc2-name-descending.png
Video qa-testing/reports/videos/issue-8-real-app-sort-video-20260702-093517.mp4
Manifest qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-8/artifacts/issue-8-real-app-video-manifest-20260702-093517.json

Bug Notes

  • No bug reproduced in installed app 1.1.2 using the Issue #8 mixed .jpg/.pts fixture.
  • Evidence was retaken with real app context and clean app-surface video after user confirmation.

Screenshots

issues.display_title: Issue #7: Fix Issue Scroll Position Resets to Top on Add/Delete in Enfuse (Auto Stitching) List
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

2026-06-23.17-51-05.mp4

Description

A highly disruptive UX issue has been reported within the Enfuse (Auto Stitching) feature. Whenever a user performs an action that updates the list-specifically adding a new image or deleting an existing 360 image-the UI forcibly resets the user's scroll position to the very top of the page.

This causes severe friction for users working with large datasets. For example, a user managing a massive stitching job with 15,000 images reported extreme frustration, as they have to manually scroll all the way back down to their previous location after every single deletion.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a user deletes an image from the Enfuse list, the image is successfully removed, and the user's scroll position remains completely stationary.
  2. When a user adds an image to the Enfuse list, the scroll position does not jump to the top.
  3. The scroll preservation works consistently, even when managing extremely large stitching jobs (e.g., thousands of images).

Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: High - highest RICE of the open defects.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
High (all heavy users) High (2x) High ~0.5 pm Quick win - ship first

Why it matters: Maps to the high-volume power-user persona - the cohort feeling the most friction and the clearest near-term retention risk. The 15k-image scenario makes this severe daily friction.

Engineering hypothesis: Likely a list-key / virtualization issue (the grid uses @tanstack/react-virtual). Suspect a non-stable React key or full list remount on add/delete that drops the virtualizer's scroll offset.

Acceptance bar = large-dataset stability, not just "it scrolls": Treat parity-at-scale as a cutover-grade gate. Verify scroll stays stationary on add and delete at thousands of items, per AC #3.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #7: Enfuse add/delete scroll preservation

Suite: installed GoThru Photo Studio Tauri real-app retest + qa-testing/automation/e2e/module/edit/enfuse/issue-7-enfuse-scroll-preservation.spec.ts
Env: Windows installed app GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2 (gothru-photo-studio-tauri.exe)
QA date: 2026-07-02
Real-app fixture shown in latest clean evidence: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-7/fixtures/enfuse-real-app-20260702-081307
Fixture size: 3,000 real JPG files loaded by the installed app
Latest clean recording manifest: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-7/artifacts/issue-7-real-app-manifest-20260702-085241.json
Video capture mode: app-surface only; excludes desktop taskbar, browser chrome, OS navbar, and unrelated windows.

Acceptance Criteria Result

Acceptance Criteria Real-app measurement Evidence Result Review
AC1: When a user deletes an image from the Enfuse list, the image is successfully removed, and the user's scroll position remains completely stationary. Browse scrollTop stayed 97530 -> 97530 after remove attempt. Queue/remove-count measurement stayed 1 -> 1, and the screenshot still shows queued rows. TC2 screenshot + clean app-surface video + manifest Fail / Not proven Scroll stayed stationary, but successful removal is not proven.
AC2: When a user adds an image to the Enfuse list, the scroll position does not jump to the top. Browse scrollTop stayed 97530 -> 97530. TC1 screenshot shows queue populated to 1000 Queue while the left browse remains around frame01950. TC1 screenshot + clean app-surface video + manifest Pass Add action did not jump the browse list to top.
AC3: The scroll preservation works consistently, even when managing extremely large stitching jobs (e.g., thousands of images). Installed app loaded 3000 files. Browse scrollTop stayed 97530 before add, after add, and after remove attempt. TC0/TC1/TC2 screenshots + clean app-surface video + manifest Partial Thousands-file scroll stability is proven, but full add/delete acceptance is not complete because delete success is not proven.

White Box Method - Source / Contract Review

Check Latest input Expected Observed Matrix Result
Scroll-reset risk Enfuse add/delete mutation path No explicit top reset or forced browse scroll reset No direct top reset was observed from real-app scroll telemetry TP Pass as supporting coverage
Acceptance gap Delete/remove behavior Removal must be measurable as item count decrease Real-app evidence did not prove count decrease FN risk Fail / Needs fix or retest

Gray Box Method - Instrumented Real-App Measurements

Check Latest input Expected Observed Matrix Result
Fixture loaded Installed app loads Issue #7 fixture Folder path and file count match test fixture fileCount=3000, first frame00000.jpg, last frame02999.jpg TP Pass
Add scroll preservation Add to Queue from mid-list Browse scroll remains stationary scrollTop 97530 -> 97530 TP Pass
Remove scroll preservation Remove queued item from mid-list Browse scroll remains stationary scrollTop 97530 -> 97530 TP Pass for scroll only
Queue count after add Add to Queue Queue/item count increases Visual TC1 shows 1000 Queue; manifest remove-button count was not a reliable queue-size metric (1 -> 1) Mixed Pass visually, metric needs improvement
Queue count after remove Remove queued item Queue/item count decreases Manifest stayed 1 -> 1; TC2 still shows queued rows FN risk Fail / Not proven

Black Box Method - Real Installed App

Step User-visible evidence Expected Observed Result
TC0: Open Enfuse on large folder Screenshot before add Real app displays Issue #7 fixture with thousands of images and a mid-list scroll position Breadcrumb shows enfuse-real-app-20260702-081307 (3000) and visible files around frame01950 Pass
TC1: Add to Queue Screenshot after add + video Queue populates and left browse list stays in place Queue shows 1000 Queue; left browse remains around frame01950 Pass
TC2: Attempt Remove/Delete Screenshot after remove + video Queue count decreases and left browse list stays in place Left browse remains around frame01950, but queued rows are still visible and item decrease is not proven Fail / Not proven

Backend / App Event Status & Review

Flow Observed status Frontend outcome Review
Installed app launch Success GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2 opened from installed executable Verified real app
Folder load 3000 files Upload/file count button shows Upload - 3000 File(s) Verified large real-app context
Enfuse queue add Visual queue populated Queue header shows 1000 Queue Add path verified visually
Enfuse remove/delete Not proven Queue rows still visible after remove attempt Do not mark passed
Scroll telemetry Stable Browse scrollTop stayed 97530 across all captured states Scroll preservation verified

Evidence Notes

  • Latest video evidence was captured from the real installed GoThru Photo Studio Tauri app as app-surface video only, so it does not include desktop/navbar/browser chrome.
  • The test used 3,000 real JPG files in the filesystem to cover the thousands-of-images acceptance scope.
  • Evidence was uploaded through qa-testing/upload-evidence.js using GitHub file attachment flow to avoid the 10 MB 413 Payload Too Large request-body issue.

Final QA Decision

Failed / Needs fix or retest. The real-app retest proves scroll does not jump during add/remove attempts with 3,000 images, and Add to Queue is visually verified. However, AC1 is not satisfied because successful delete/remove from the Enfuse queue is not proven by the latest screenshot/video/manifest.

Screenshots

TC0 - Real app Issue #7 fixture loaded in Enfuse before queue add:

TC1 - Real app Enfuse queue after Add to Queue:

TC2 - Real app Enfuse queue after attempted Remove:

Video Recording

issues.display_title: Issue #9: Fix Issue Pressing F5 Did Not Work in File Manager
issues.main_description_html:
Demo by developer

Screen.Recording.2026-06-23.at.15.57.53.mov

Pressing F5 does not refresh the directory listing in the File Manager. The keyboard shortcut appears to be unbound or not wired to the refresh action.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Pressing F5 while the File Manager is focused refreshes the current directory listing.
  2. The refresh reflects files added/removed on disk since the last load.

📊 Product analysis context (from docs/projectbase/analyze.md)

Priority: 🟡 Medium — Quick win.

Reach Impact Confidence Effort Verdict
Med Med (1×) High ~0.25 pm Quick win — cheapest of the three

Engineering hypothesis: Cheap keybinding wiring — F5 appears unbound or not mapped to the File Manager refresh action. Restores expected desktop behavior. Confirm the hotkey is registered through the existing settings/hotkeys path rather than hardcoded.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #9: Fix Issue Pressing F5 Did Not Work in File Manager

Suite: qa-testing/scripts/record-issue-9-real-app-cdp.cjs (Real installed Tauri app via WebView2 CDP)
Env: GoThru Photo Studio Tauri 1.1.2 installed app (C:\Users\Work\AppData\Local\GoThru Photo Studio\gothru-photo-studio-tauri.exe)
Test date: 2026-07-02 16:50 WIB
Fixture: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-9/fixtures/f5-refresh-20260702-095024
Evidence manifest: qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-9/artifacts/issue-9-real-app-manifest-20260702-095024.json
Video: Not recorded for this retest. Per saved QA preference, video recording requires explicit confirmation first.

Test Scenario & Result

# Method Scenario Given -> Expected Actual Matrix Result
1 White Box F5 shortcut wiring review F5 should invoke File Manager refresh/load for the focused panel Renderer code handles f5-pressed by calling fileManagerRef.current.copySources(); File Manager UI labels Copy as (F5) FP Fail
2 Gray Box Real app File Manager focused, press F5 Active File Manager panel at Issue #9 fixture -> pressing F5 should trigger a new load-files event for current directory load-files event count stayed 2 -> 2 after F5; no explicit refresh event was observed FN Fail
3 Gray Box Disk changes after last load Add added-after-load.jpg and remove remove-me.jpg, then refresh should reflect added/removed files UI showed added-after-load.jpg and hid remove-me.jpg, but this happened before F5 through existing directory update behavior, not because F5 triggered refresh TP for visual disk reflection, but not attributable to F5 Partial
4 Black Box End-user visual evidence context Screenshots must show real app File Manager and Issue #9 fixture before/after F5 Screenshots show File Manager active and the fixture path f5-refresh-20260702-095024 TP Pass

Acceptance Criteria Review

Acceptance Criteria Evidence Result
Pressing F5 while the File Manager is focused refreshes the current directory listing After F5, observed load-files event count did not increase (2 -> 2). Code path also maps F5 to Copy, not reload. Fail
The refresh reflects files added/removed on disk since the last load Added/removed files were reflected visually, but the manifest shows no F5-triggered refresh event. This behavior appears to come from existing watcher/auto-update, so AC is not fully proven as an F5 refresh. Partial / Needs fix

Backend / IPC Status Review

Channel / Layer Input Observed Output Review
Initial renderer IPC load-files viewer: file-manager, panelSide: left, fixture path Payload contained alpha-existing.jpg, remove-me.jpg Verified
Disk mutation Add added-after-load.jpg; remove remove-me.jpg Visual UI later showed added-after-load.jpg, alpha-existing.jpg Verified visually
Keyboard F5 Focus app body/File Manager and press F5 No new load-files event observed after keypress Failed
Code path src/renderer/components/core/Content.tsx F5 handler f5-pressed calls fileManagerRef.current.copySources() Failed wiring

Evidence Files

Type File
Screenshot qa-testing/reports/screenshots/issue-9-real-app-f5-refresh-20260702-095024-tc0-initial-load.png
Screenshot qa-testing/reports/screenshots/issue-9-real-app-f5-refresh-20260702-095024-tc1-disk-mutated-before-f5.png
Screenshot qa-testing/reports/screenshots/issue-9-real-app-f5-refresh-20260702-095024-tc2-after-f5-keypress.png
Manifest qa-testing/evidence/issues/issue-9/artifacts/issue-9-real-app-manifest-20260702-095024.json

Bug Notes

  • Issue reproduced in installed app 1.1.2.
  • F5 is currently wired to Copy behavior, not File Manager reload/refresh.
  • Recommended fix direction: when layout.viewerType === 'file-manager', F5 should call the same reload logic as the File Manager refresh button for the active panel, preserving current directory, panel side, external flag, and sort config.

Screenshots

TC0 - Real app File Manager initial load before disk change:

TC1 - Real app File Manager after disk add/remove before F5:

TC2 - Real app File Manager after pressing F5:

issues.display_title: Issue #10: [Audit] Tauri App Feature Validation & Cross-check
issues.main_description_html:
Goal

Validate that every migrated feature in the Tauri v2 build behaves at parity with the original Electron app, and cross-check the migration inventory against what is actually implemented and working.

Scope

Full feature sweep across the app, cross-referenced with docs/migration/00-ipc-inventory.md (142 IPC channels) and the 11 per-feature docs in docs/migration/features/.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Each feature area is validated against its docs/migration/features/*.md contract and marked pass/fail.
  2. Any parity gaps or regressions are filed as linked follow-up issues.
  3. The IPC inventory status (✅ / 🔵 / ⬜) is reconciled with actual implementation state.

Context: see docs/projectbase/analyze.md — the project is in a stabilization/cutover phase; this audit feeds Phase 4 cutover readiness.

Completed

  • Optimized Browse
  • Fixed Copy & Move in File Manager
  • Image Centering
  • Upload to GoThru
  • Upload to Panorra
  • Upload to StreetView
  • Fixed error in Enfuse
  • Duplicate feature
  • Optimized Sorting
  • Copy Path in File Manager
  • Create Folder
  • Fixed flickering on Browse
  • Subcription
  • Clean up code on some scripts

🧪 QA Tester Summary — Issue #10: Tauri App Feature Validation & Cross-check

Suite: static migration audit + qa-testing/automation/e2e/module/migration/issue-6-tauri-migration-scoped-windows.spec.ts
Env: Windows workspace / installed-app config GoThru Photo Studio 1.1.2
Target: migration inventory, feature docs, frontend type/build gates, scoped migration automation
Build/Version: repo 869ad84, package 1.1.0
Tester: Codex
Date: 2026-07-02

✅ Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given → Expected Matrix Result
1 White ipc_inventory_reconciliation docs/migration/00-ipc-inventory.md → every high-risk migrated channel has an accurate status TP ❌ Fail
2 White feature_doc_contracts 11 feature docs → each feature has usable UX/output parity contract evidence TP ⚠️ Conditional Fail
3 Gray scoped_migration_contract Tauri shell/bridge/config → migration contract checks pass TP ✅ Pass
4 Gray type_and_build_gates current workspace → typecheck and production build pass TP ❌ Fail
5 Black stale_reference_check Issue #10 context docs → every referenced file exists in checkout TN ❌ Fail
6 Task Sprint #10 acceptance full feature sweep + parity gaps filed + IPC inventory reconciled TP + TN ⛔ Blocked / Not ready

Typecheck Failure Summary

Area Representative errors
Edit / Preview Browse IFile passed to never in selected-file paths
Room Staging nullable furniture state inferred as null / never
Virtual Staging 2D missing exported VirtualStaging2DFurniture type from @types
File Manager missing module ../../../types/utils from OverwriteFileModal.tsx
Map GeoJSON Feature is missing required properties
Header `string

Risk & Coverage Notes

  • Covered: static inventory reconciliation, feature-doc presence check, scoped migration Playwright contract, frontend production build, TypeScript typecheck, visual installed-app smoke evidence.
  • Not covered: full installed-app manual sweep for every feature area, Electron baseline comparison, pixel/byte/EXIF golden-output parity, live upload/auth/payment/API flows, macOS runtime.
  • Skipped/Blocked: full installed-app feature sweep was not executed; the uploaded visual evidence is a smoke/state recording, not a complete feature parity walkthrough.
  • Residual risk: High. Current evidence supports keeping Issue #10 open until typecheck is fixed, H-risk inventory rows are reconciled, and per-feature parity evidence is attached.

QA Decision

Result: ❌ Fail / blocked for closure
Recommendation: Keep Issue #10 open and retest after #32, #51, #52, #53, and #54 are resolved.
